content: Rebecca, this is exactly what I try to tell our congregation. You do not need a sermon. You need to show up. Thank you for putting it so plainly.
I read this right after visiting my father in the hospital. Thank you for the permission to just be there.
That is exactly why I wrote it. Praying for you and your father.
Patsy Cline came up more than once - because of course she did. Great episode, Bec. See you at Cafe Eclectic.
The still waters line stopped me cold. That is the tension I sit in every day. Thank you for naming it.
Heard you read this on the air Saturday and came straight to the site. Sharing this with my grief support group.
The light shining in the darkness, and the darkness not overcoming it. I will be borrowing that for Sunday. With full credit, of course.
All yours, Pastor Greer. It is right there in John 1 - I just had to point.
Angela's line about diagnosis as a chapter gave me chills. Please have her on again.
Thank you, Deacon Mosley. Rebecca creates the kind of space where you can say the real thing.
We printed this prayer out and taped it to the wall at our community center. Thank you.
Angela, this is beautifully said. I teach my nursing students about accompaniment all the time. Glad this is on the site.
Already planning to volunteer at the health fair. Deacon Mosley mentioned the glucose screenings specifically - much needed in our neighborhood.
This explains so much about why your show prep is always so good. The pages show up in the broadcast.
You said the real thing on that episode, sis. Not many people are willing to hold the complicated ones.
My cat and my porch and a Saturday morning - you just described my whole spiritual life right now. Thank you.
Thank you for this. Being known versus being cared for - I am printing this for my supervisor session.
